text:
COMPREHENSIVE GENOMIC PROFILING REPORT
SOLID TUMOR PANEL вЂ” NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING

в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђ
PATIENT INFORMATION
в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђ

Date of Birth: [ON FILE]
Sex: Female
Ordering Physician: [Head and Neck Oncology / ENT]
Institution: [Institutional Header]
Specimen Accession #: [LAB-ACCESSION-ON-FILE]
Report ID: NGS-[REPORT-ID-ON-FILE]

в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђ
SPECIMEN INFORMATION
в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђ
Specimen Type: Formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded (FFPE) tissue
Anatomic Site: Right base of tongue (primary tumor)
Procedure: Direct laryngoscopy with biopsy
Tumor Histology: Squamous cell carcinoma, HPV-associated (p16 positive)
Tumor Purity (Pathologist Estimate): 60% (adequate for analysis)
Nucleic Acid Quality: PASS вЂ” DNA yield 142 ng; RNA yield 38 ng
Panel: Solid Tumor Comprehensive Panel (523-gene DNA + fusion RNA panel)
Sequencing Platform: Illumina NovaSeq 6000
Mean Coverage Depth (DNA): 512x
Percent Bases в‰Ґ100x Coverage: 98.7%

в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђ
QUALITY CONTROL SUMMARY
в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђ
Overall QC Status: PASS
DNA Quality: PASS
RNA Quality: PASS (DV200: 74%)
Sequencing QC: PASS
Note: Results are interpreted in the context of the clinical and pathologic diagnosis. All reported variants have been evaluated for potential germline origin; if clinical concern for hereditary susceptibility, germline testing is recommended.

в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђ
CLINICAL INDICATION
в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђ
Stage III (T2N1M0) HPV-positive (p16+, HPV-16) squamous cell carcinoma of the right base of tongue, oropharynx. Newly diagnosed. Comprehensive genomic profiling requested to characterize tumor molecular profile, identify actionable alterations, and inform therapeutic decision-making.

в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђ
KEY BIOMARKER SUMMARY (TOP-LINE)
в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђ
  Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB):       5 mut/Mb   вЂ”   LOW
  Microsatellite Status (MSI):         MSS (Microsatellite Stable)
  PD-L1 Expression (CPS, IHC 22C3):   12   вЂ”   POSITIVE
  HPV Status (by sequencing):          HPV-16 DETECTED; genomic integration confirmed
  TP53:                                WILD-TYPE (not mutated)
  PIK3CA:                              MUTATION DETECTED вЂ” p.E545K (Exon 9, Helical Domain) вЂ” POTENTIALLY ACTIONABLE

в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђ
SOMAT IC MUTATIONS вЂ” DETAILED FINDINGS
в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђ

--- TIER 1 / POTENTIALLY ACTIONABLE ALTERATIONS ---

1. PIK3CA вЂ” p.E545K (c.1633G>A)
   Gene Function:  Phosphatidylinositol-4,5-bisphosphate 3-kinase catalytic subunit alpha; PI3K/AKT/mTOR pathway
   Variant Class:  Missense, Exon 9 (helical domain)
   Variant Allele Frequency (VAF): 28%
   Read Support:   Alt/Total: 143/511
   Predicted Effect: Activating вЂ” E545K is a well-characterized hotspot mutation in the helical domain of PIK3CA. Disrupts inhibitory interaction with the regulatory p85 subunit (PIK3R1), leading to constitutive PI3K signaling and downstream AKT/mTOR activation independent of RTK input.
   Clinical Significance: PIK3CA E545K is among the most frequently observed activating mutations in HPV-positive oropharyngeal SCC (reported in 15вЂ“25% of cases), consistent with the TP53-wild-type/HPV-driven carcinogenetic background of this tumor. This variant is associated with sensitivity to PI3K pathway inhibitors (e.g., alpelisib, copanlisib) and mTOR inhibitors in selected tumor types, though clinical evidence in OPSCC is investigational. No approved PI3K-targeted therapy exists for OPSCC at this time; enrollment in clinical trials targeting the PI3K/mTOR pathway may be appropriate.
   FDA Approval in This Tumor Type: None (investigational)
   Clinical Trials: NCT trials evaluating PI3K/AKT/mTOR inhibitors in HNSCC available; consider clinical trial registry search

--- TIER 2 / MUTATIONS OF UNCERTAIN OR LOW CLINICAL SIGNIFICANCE ---

2. FAT1 вЂ” p.R1837* (c.5509C>T)
   Gene Function:  FAT atypical cadherin 1; tumor suppressor involved in Hippo pathway regulation and cell polarity
   Variant Class:  Nonsense (stop-gain), Exon 15
   VAF: 18% | Read Support: Alt/Total: 92/511
   Predicted Effect: Loss-of-function (truncation). FAT1 inactivation is common in head and neck SCC (reported in up to 30% of cases), with functional studies suggesting roles in Hippo/YAP1 pathway dysregulation and Wnt pathway crosstalk. No direct targeted therapy available. Emerging preclinical data suggest potential synthetic lethal interactions, but no clinical actionability at this time.
   Clinical Significance: Tier 2 вЂ” variant of uncertain clinical significance; biologically consistent with HNSCC genomic landscape.

3. KMT2D вЂ” p.Q1389fs (c.4165_4166del)
   Gene Function:  Lysine methyltransferase 2D; histone H3K4 mono- and di-methyltransferase; chromatin remodeling and transcriptional regulation
   Variant Class:  Frameshift deletion, Exon 39
   VAF: 22% | Read Support: Alt/Total: 112/511
   Predicted Effect: Loss-of-function (frameshift leading to premature termination). KMT2D is among the most frequently mutated chromatin remodeling genes in HNSCC and other squamous cell carcinomas. Inactivating mutations are associated with aberrant transcriptional programs and may contribute to epigenetic reprogramming in HPV-driven tumors. Emerging data suggest KMT2D-mutant tumors may have altered sensitivity to immune checkpoint inhibition, though this is not established clinically.
   Clinical Significance: Tier 2 вЂ” biologically relevant, no direct approved targeted therapy.

4. NOTCH1 вЂ” p.P2415fs (c.7243_7244insA)
   Gene Function:  Notch receptor 1; transmembrane receptor involved in cell fate, differentiation, and tumor suppression in squamous epithelium
   Variant Class:  Frameshift insertion, Exon 34 (PEST domain)
   VAF: 15% | Read Support: Alt/Total: 77/511
   Predicted Effect: Loss-of-function. NOTCH1 PEST domain mutations are well established in HNSCC as inactivating alterations that abrogate proteasomal degradation of activated NOTCH1 intracellular domain; paradoxically, NOTCH1 functions as a tumor suppressor in stratified squamous epithelium (in contrast to its oncogenic role in lymphoid malignancies). Consistent with the genomic pattern of HPV-positive OPSCC. NOTCH1 loss-of-function does not have a currently approved targeted therapeutic intervention.
   Clinical Significance: Tier 2 вЂ” loss-of-function consistent with HNSCC, uncertain individual clinical significance.

5. EP300 вЂ” p.R240H (c.719G>A)
   Gene Function:  E1A binding protein p300; histone acetyltransferase (HAT); transcriptional coactivator; chromatin remodeling
   Variant Class:  Missense, Exon 9 (TAZ2/KIX domain)
   VAF: 12% | Read Support: Alt/Total: 61/511
   Predicted Effect: Likely loss-of-function or partial functional impairment вЂ” R240 is within the KIX domain involved in coactivator protein interactions. EP300 mutations are observed in HNSCC and various carcinomas, often resulting in altered p53 coactivation and aberrant transcriptional regulation. No direct targeted therapy approved. EP300/CREBBP alterations have been associated with enhanced sensitivity to BET bromodomain inhibitors and HDAC inhibitors in preclinical models.
   Clinical Significance: Tier 2 вЂ” variant of uncertain significance; biologically plausible loss-of-function.

в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђ
NOTABLE WILD-TYPE / NOT DETECTED FINDINGS
в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђ
TP53:    NOT MUTATED (wild-type)
         Interpretation: TP53 wild-type status is characteristic of HPV-driven oropharyngeal carcinoma. HPV oncoprotein E6 targets p53 for proteasomal degradation, rendering somatic TP53 mutation redundant in the carcinogenic process. This finding supports the HPV-positive classification and is prognostically favorable relative to HPV-negative, TP53-mutant HNSCC.

KRAS / HRAS / NRAS: NOT DETECTED (wild-type)
         No activating RAS family mutations identified. Consistent with squamous cell carcinoma histology (RAS mutations are rare in HNSCC).

CDKN2A (p16/ARF): NOT DELETED
         No homozygous or heterozygous deletion of CDKN2A detected. Note: p16 protein overexpression in this tumor is driven by HPV E7-mediated Rb inactivation (transcriptional upregulation of CDKN2A/p16), not by genomic loss вЂ” consistent with immunohistochemical p16 positivity (3+, >75% staining).

FGFR2 / FGFR3 / FGFR4: NOT MUTATED, NOT FUSED
         FGFR1 low-level copy gain noted (see copy number section). No kinase domain mutations or activating fusions in FGFR family genes.

EGFR: NOT MUTATED; polysomy present (see copy number section)
MET: NOT AMPLIFIED, NOT MUTATED
BRAF: NOT MUTATED
PIK3R1 / AKT1 / PTEN: NOT MUTATED (no additional PI3K pathway co-mutations; PTEN copy number neutral)

в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђ
COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS (CNA)
в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђ

1. CCND1 (Cyclin D1) вЂ” FOCAL GAIN, Chromosome 11q13
   Copy Number Estimate: ~4вЂ“5 copies (low-level gain; not high-level amplification)
   Interpretation: 11q13 amplification encompassing CCND1 is the most common copy number event in HNSCC, reported in 20вЂ“30% of cases. Cyclin D1 overexpression promotes CDK4/6-mediated Rb phosphorylation and cell cycle progression. Low-level gain (as opposed to high-level amplification в‰Ґ6 copies) is less reliably associated with response to CDK4/6 inhibitors. No approved CDK4/6 inhibitor in HNSCC; investigational context only.

2. FGFR1 вЂ” LOW-LEVEL GAIN, Chromosome 8p11.23
   Copy Number Estimate: ~3 copies (low-level gain)
   Interpretation: Low-level FGFR1 copy gain is observed in a subset of squamous cell carcinomas. In the absence of high-level amplification (в‰Ґ6 copies) or activating mutation/fusion, predictive value for FGFR inhibitor response is limited. No actionable threshold met at this time.

3. EGFR вЂ” POLYSOMY (low-level gain), Chromosome 7p11.2
   Copy Number Estimate: ~3вЂ“4 copies (polysomy; not high-level amplification)
   Interpretation: EGFR polysomy is common in HNSCC and does not meet criteria for EGFR high-level amplification or gene amplification-driven cetuximab predictive biomarker status. EGFR protein overexpression by IHC (not performed on this specimen; clinical correlation recommended if cetuximab-based therapy is considered).

   NO HIGH-LEVEL AMPLIFICATIONS DETECTED in any gene on the panel.
   NO HOMOZYGOUS DELETIONS DETECTED (including CDKN2A, PTEN, RB1, SMAD4).

в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђ
GENE FUSIONS AND STRUCTURAL VARIANTS
в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђ
No actionable or pathogenic gene fusions detected by RNA-based fusion panel.
NKX2-1, RET, NTRK1/2/3, ALK, ROS1, MET, FGFR fusions: NOT DETECTED.
No structural rearrangements of therapeutic significance identified.

в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђ
VIRAL INTEGRATION ANALYSIS
в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђ
HPV-16 Genomic Integration: DETECTED
  HPV-16 viral reads identified: Present (high read depth, >500x coverage at viral loci)
  Integration Pattern: HPV-16 sequences detected with evidence of integration into the host genome (hybrid viral-human junction reads identified). Integration sites mapped near chromosomal region 8q24 (MYC locus region) and intergenic region 3q26 вЂ” integration sites may have implications for local gene dysregulation.
  HPV-16 E6/E7 oncogene coverage: High-depth coverage of E6 and E7 ORFs; viral oncoproteins presumed transcriptionally active, consistent with HPV-driven carcinogenesis.
  HPV Genotype: HPV type 16 (high-risk alpha-papillomavirus genotype)
  Other HPV genotypes: NOT DETECTED
  Interpretation: HPV-16 genomic integration is consistent with the established diagnosis of HPV-associated oropharyngeal SCC and is consistent with the TP53 wild-type, p16-overexpressing phenotype. HPV-positive OPSCC has a distinct and more favorable genomic landscape compared to HPV-negative HNSCC, with lower overall mutational burden and characteristic absence of TP53 and CDKN2A alterations.

в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђ
IMMUNOTHERAPY BIOMARKERS
в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђ
PD-L1 Expression (IHC, Dako 22C3 pharmDx assay вЂ” performed concurrently on diagnostic specimen):
  Combined Positive Score (CPS): 12
  Tumor Proportion Score (TPS): 8%
  Interpretation: PD-L1 CPS 12 is POSITIVE (CPS в‰Ґ1). In the metastatic/recurrent HNSCC setting, pembrolizumab (Keytruda) monotherapy is approved for CPS в‰Ґ1 tumors, and pembrolizumab + chemotherapy for CPS в‰Ґ1 tumors. PD-L1 CPS в‰Ґ20 defines the highest-benefit subgroup for pembrolizumab monotherapy (CPS 12 falls in the intermediate positive category). This information is provided for completeness; for stage III resectable/locally advanced disease treated with definitive chemoradiation, PD-L1 CPS is currently investigational rather than treatment-directive.

TMB (Tumor Mutational Burden): 5 mutations/Mb
  Classification: LOW (TMB-Low, <10 mut/Mb)
  Interpretation: Low TMB is characteristic of HPV-positive oropharyngeal SCC and reflects the fact that viral-driven carcinogenesis (via HPV oncoproteins E6/E7) requires fewer somatic mutational events than carcinogen-driven (tobacco/alcohol-associated) HNSCC. Low TMB is not predictive of response to immune checkpoint inhibition in a simple linear fashion, but FDA pembrolizumab TMB-H approval (в‰Ґ10 mut/Mb) does not apply here.

Microsatellite Instability (MSI): MICROSATELLITE STABLE (MSS)
  Analysis: Evaluation of 114 microsatellite loci; no instability detected.
  Interpretation: MSS status. Pembrolizumab approval based on MSI-H/dMMR does not apply to this tumor.

в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђ
GENOMIC SUMMARY AND INTERPRETIVE COMMENT
в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђ
This tumor demonstrates a genomic profile highly consistent with HPV-positive oropharyngeal squamous cell carcinoma. Hallmark features include:

  (1) TP53 wild-type status вЂ” reflecting HPV E6-mediated p53 protein degradation rather than somatic mutation, a defining feature of HPV-driven OPSCC
  (2) Intact CDKN2A вЂ” p16 overexpression in this tumor is a consequence of Rb inactivation by HPV E7, not genomic CDKN2A loss
  (3) Low tumor mutational burden (5 mut/Mb) вЂ” characteristic of virally-driven rather than carcinogen-driven tumorigenesis
  (4) PIK3CA E545K activating mutation вЂ” the PI3K pathway is among the most commonly altered in HPV-positive OPSCC; this helical domain hotspot mutation is of potential therapeutic interest
  (5) Chromatin remodeling gene alterations (KMT2D frameshift, EP300 missense) вЂ” frequently co-occurring in HNSCC, reflecting epigenetic dysregulation
  (6) NOTCH1 and FAT1 loss-of-function mutations вЂ” consistent with the known tumor suppressor roles of these genes in squamous epithelium
  (7) Low-level copy gains in CCND1 (11q13), FGFR1 (8p11), and EGFR polysomy вЂ” collectively common in HNSCC; no high-level amplifications
  (8) Confirmed HPV-16 genomic integration вЂ” supporting active viral oncogenesis
  (9) PD-L1 CPS 12 вЂ” intermediate positive, potentially relevant in recurrent/metastatic setting

No tier 1 FDA-approved targeted therapies are currently indicated for this patient in the frontline locally advanced setting based on the identified genomic alterations. The PIK3CA E545K mutation represents the most clinically relevant finding for potential future therapeutic targeting, particularly if systemic therapy becomes indicated in recurrent or metastatic disease.

This genomic profile does not alter the standard-of-care recommendation for definitive concurrent chemoradiation (cisplatin-based) for stage III HPV-positive OPSCC, which remains the evidence-based treatment approach.

Clinical trial considerations: Trials evaluating PI3K/mTOR pathway inhibitors in HNSCC, trials incorporating checkpoint inhibitors (anti-PD-1/PD-L1) in combination with definitive chemoradiation, and epigenetic targeting strategies may be relevant for this patient in appropriate disease contexts.

в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђ
METHODS
в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђ
DNA was extracted from macro-dissected FFPE tumor tissue with confirmed в‰Ґ20% tumor nuclei. Library preparation was performed using a hybridization capture-based approach (523 cancer-relevant genes, including full exon coverage of all listed genes plus selected introns for fusion detection). Sequencing was performed on Illumina NovaSeq 6000. Bioinformatic analysis included alignment to hg19, variant calling using validated somatic variant callers with matched normal (patient PBMC-derived germline comparator), copy number analysis using an in-house panel of normals baseline, and fusion detection from RNA-seq data. Variants with VAF <5% or read depth <50x were excluded from reporting. Germline filtering applied; variants with >1% population allele frequency in gnomAD were classified as likely germline and not reported as somatic unless known oncogenic hotspots. MSI analysis performed using 114 microsatellite loci. TMB calculated from exonic somatic mutations per megabase of sequenced coding genome. PD-L1 IHC (Dako 22C3 pharmDx) performed independently by anatomic pathology on a representative FFPE section.

Assay Sensitivity: VAF в‰Ґ5% with в‰Ґ50x coverage for SNVs/indels; copy number changes в‰Ґ1.5-fold; fusions supported by в‰Ґ5 spanning reads.
